Phone: +1 713 234 0863

Software logging and importing data into Excel

Modified on: Fri, 3 Jul, 2020 at 7:10 PM

Log File Description

The Rheonics Control Panel Software creates the following files while logging:

Name

EG 001_debug.txt

filename_log.txt: main log file containing the measured values
filename_events.txt: user-entered events log
filename_debug.txt: logs the output stream of the electronics unit
MMDDYYYY_hhmm_xy_log.txt: software-created copy of the currently logging file.

Note: the columns in the file are separated by tab stops and space. When importing the file in excel, please select tab and space as the delimiters.

filename_log.txt

Example of a log file –opened in excel. Note: data is separated by tabs.
key_facts: The Rheonics Control Panel Software generates various log files.

Log files include measured values (filename_log.txt), user-entered events (filename_events.txt), output stream of electronics unit (filename_debug.txt), and a software-created copy of the currently logging file (MMDDYYYY_hhmm_xy_log.txt).
Log file columns are separated by tab stops and spaces.
When importing log files into Excel, tab and space should be selected as delimiters.
Data within log files opened in Excel is separated by tabs.
warnings_notes: Note: the columns in the file are separated by tab stops and space. When importing the file in excel, please select tab and space as the delimiters.
Note: data is separated by tabs.

chunks.page_summary: This page details the structure of Rheonics measurement log files and provides instructions for importing them into Microsoft Excel for analysis.
chunks.extracted_text:
Start Log: 28.08.2015 14:12
Sensor: DVM
Serial Number: D02-007
eBox Serial Number: D02-007 SWV9.04 ESNE03-1124
Sample Name: EG 001
Communication mode: Ethernet (10.5.33.98:10001)
Operator Name: Rheonics
Operator Client: Rheonics GmbH
Measurement Algorithm: eBox

Date
TimeStamp Abs
TimeStamp Rel [s]
Viscosity [mPa.s]
Density [g/cc]
Temperature [°C]
Pressure [bar]
Fres [Hz]
dF [Hz]
Channel 1 [mA]
Channel 2 [mA]
Event [id]
Event [time]

The log file filename_log.txt contains the following:

Header of measurement: Information about the measurement settings
Data heading row showing the sequence of data values:

* Date: Date of measurement
* Timestamp absolute: Computer time for the measurement
* Timestamp relative [s]: Relative time in seconds from the start of logging
* Viscosity [units]: Measured viscosity with selected units
* Density [units]: Measured density
* Temperature [units]: Measured temperature
* Pressure [units]: Pressure (manual or read from externally connected gauge)
* Fres [Hz]: Sensor resonant frequency
* dF [Hz]: Sensor damping
* Channel 1 [mA]: Channel 1 current loop 4-20mA input reading (only old e-box)
* Channel 2 [mA]: Channel 2 current loop 4-20mA input reading (only old e-box)
* Event [id]: User-entered event identification name
* Event [time]: Timestamp of entered Event

Importing files in Excel

1. Filename_log.txt
   a. Import data ‘From text’
   b. Select file type ‘Delimited’
   c. Select delimiters
      i. Tab
2. Filename_debug.txt
   a. Import data ‘From text’
   b. Select file type ‘Delimited’
key_facts: Rheonics measurement log files (`filename_log.txt` and `filename_debug.txt`) contain detailed measurement data and settings.
Log files include header information about the measurement setup, such as sensor and eBox serial numbers, sample name, and communication mode.
Measurement data columns typically include date, absolute and relative timestamps, viscosity, density, temperature, pressure, sensor resonant frequency (Fres), and sensor damping (dF).
The system supports logging 4-20mA current loop input readings for Channel 1 and Channel 2, specifically for old e-box models.
Users can log custom events with an identification name and timestamp.
Log files are typically tab-delimited text files that can be imported into Excel by selecting 'From text' and 'Delimited' options with 'Tab' as the delimiter.
warnings_notes: Channel 1 current loop 4-20mA input reading is only applicable to old e-box models.
Channel 2 current loop 4-20mA input reading is only applicable to old e-box models.

key_facts: Delimiters can be selected as Tab or Space.
The file type 'Delimited' should be selected for import.
For 'Filename_events.txt', the delimiter to select is 'Tab'.
